Enhanced description clarifies what data is returned (annual performance metrics, returns, comparative analysis), what inputs are expected (metal type, date range), authentication requirements, and the operation's use case. This helps AI agents understand the operation's purpose and when to invoke it.

data.current_summary: Get Historical Annual Performance for requested metals
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves annual historical performance metrics for specified precious metals. Returns performance data aggregated by year, including returns and comparative analysis. Requires authentication and accepts parameters to filter by metal type and date range. Useful for analyzing long-term metal price trends and year-over-year performance comparisons.
data.reasoning: Current description 'Annual Historical Performance information' is too vague and provides no actionable details. Enhanced description clarifies what data is returned (annual performance metrics, returns, comparative analysis), what inputs are expected (metal type, date range), authentication requirements, and the operation's use case. This helps AI agents understand the operation's purpose and when to invoke it.

message: Operation 'Currencies_SupportedCurrencies_Summary_GET' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details. Description lacks clarity about what is returned and doesn't explain why direction matters. Enhanced description clarifies the return value, explains the directional significance, and provides actionable guidance for AI agents on how to use this information.

data.current_summary: Get list of currency pairs supported by the Summary endpoint
data.current_description:
Only the currency pairs in the direction noted can be used with the Summary endpoint.
For example: USD/CAD is not the same as CAD/USD
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the list of currency pairs supported by the Summary endpoint. Currency pair direction is significant - USD/CAD and CAD/USD are treated as distinct pairs. Returns a collection of supported currency pairs in their valid directional format. Use this to validate currency pair inputs before calling the Summary endpoint.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity about what is returned and doesn't explain why direction matters. Enhanced description clarifies the return value, explains the directional significance, and provides actionable guidance for AI agents on how to use this information.

data.current_summary: Get historical prices for requested currency pairs
data.current_description:
Historical OHLC data for the specified period and interval size 

The combination of the interval parameter and start and end dates can result in results
being truncated to conform to result size limits. See comments on interval parameter for details on valid interval values.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves historical OHLC (Open, High, Low, Close) data for specified currency pairs within a date range. Accepts currency pair(s), start date, end date, and interval size as parameters. Returns a collection of interval-based price data points. Results may be truncated based on result size limits and interval selection. Requires authentication token. Refer to interval parameter documentation for valid interval values.
data.reasoning: Description mentions result truncation but lacks clarity on inputs and complete output structure. Enhanced description explicitly lists expected parameters, clarifies the OHLC data structure, explains truncation behavior more clearly, notes authentication requirement, and provides better guidance for AI agents on parameter selection.

message: Operation 'Metals_SpotHistory_GET' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Summary is clear and actionable. Description contains good technical detail but lacks clarity on input parameters, response format, and how to interpret the historicalfx flag. Reorganized for better readability and added response schema reference and authentication requirement.

data.current_summary: Get historical Spot prices for requested metals
data.current_description:
Historical OHLC data for the specified period and interval size 

The combination of the interval parameter and start and end dates can result in results
being truncated to conform to result size limits. See comments on interval parameter for details on valid interval values.
 
The historicalfx flag is used to determine whether to apply today's fx rates to a historical period, or to apply the historical rates from that same time period.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves historical OHLC (Open, High, Low, Close) spot price data for requested metals over a specified period and interval. Accepts parameters: metal symbols, start/end dates, interval size, and historicalfx flag to control FX rate application (current rates vs. historical rates from the period). Returns an IntervalCollectionResponse with historical price intervals. Note: Results may be truncated based on result size limits; refer to interval parameter documentation for valid values. Requires authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description contains good technical detail but lacks clarity on input parameters, response format, and how to interpret the historicalfx flag. Reorganized for better readability and added response schema reference and authentication requirement.

data.current_description:
Historical data for the specified period and interval size
 
The combination of the interval parameter and start and end dates can result in results

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves historical spot price ratio data for requested metal pairs over a specified period and interval.
